findings.title: Reverts autoescape fix back onto deprecated Starlette **env_options path — forward-compat XSS regression
findings.problem: This diff reverts commit e11187e ('fix: use preconfigured jinja2.Environment for autoescape'), whose commit message explicitly states: 'Starlette removed the **env_options passthrough in Jinja2Templates, breaking autoescape=True on newer versions.' The new line `Jinja2Templates(directory=str(TEMPLATES_DIR), autoescape=True)` passes `autoescape` through `**env_options`, which Starlette 0.52.1 (the pinned version, uv.lock) now emits a DeprecationWarning for: 'Extra environment options are deprecated. Use a preconfigured jinja2.Environment instead.' Verified on the pinned version that autoescape STILL works today (env.autoescape=True; '<script>' renders as '<script>'), so this is not a live vuln right now — but it re-introduces the exact deprecated path the prior commit removed. On a future Starlette upgrade that drops **env_options, this line either raises TypeError or silently drops autoescape (raw jinja2.Environment defaults autoescape=False), which would expose stored/reflected XSS across every dashboard template that renders user-controlled values. The project CLAUDE.md lists 'never disable autoescaping' as an absolute security rule. pytest has no `filterwarnings=error`, so this regression is invisible to the test suite.
findings.fix_hint: Keep the preconfigured jinja2.Environment form from e11187e (env=jinja2.Environment(loader=FileSystemLoader(...), autoescape=True)); it is the supported, non-deprecated API and pins autoescape regardless of Starlette version.
findings.why_it_matters: The whole web UI renders through this single templates object; if a Starlette bump ever removes the deprecated passthrough and autoescape silently falls back to False, every template becomes an XSS sink at once. This diff knowingly walks back a fix that was made precisely to avoid that.

findings.title: Diff reverts commit e11187e, reintroducing Jinja2Templates(directory=..., autoescape=True) in place of the preconfigured jinja2.Environment pattern that commit introduced to keep autoescape reliably enabled
findings.problem: CLAUDE.md (root): 'in Jinja2, never apply `| safe` to user content or disable autoescaping.' The prior fix commit's message states verbatim: 'Starlette removed the **env_options passthrough in Jinja2Templates, breaking autoescape=True on newer versions. Pass a preconfigured jinja2.Environment instead, which is the supported API.' This diff undoes exactly that fix.
findings.why_it_matters: Tested against the currently pinned Starlette 0.52.1 (uv.lock): `Jinja2Templates(directory=..., autoescape=True)` still sets `env.autoescape == True` — the `**env_options` passthrough still functionally works, it only emits a DeprecationWarning. So autoescaping is NOT currently disabled by this diff on the pinned Starlette version. However, the passthrough is explicitly deprecated Starlette behavior; a future release fully removing it would silently flip autoescape to Jinja2's default of False for every Jinja2Templates response in this app, re-opening stored/reflected XSS via unescaped template output. A real regression of a security-motivated fix, verified to be currently latent rather than currently live.

findings.title: Diff reverts commit e11187e, reintroducing the exact autoescape fragility that commit was written to fix, and reintroduces a now-deprecated Jinja2Templates call path
findings.problem: This diff is a byte-for-byte revert of the prior commit `e11187e fix: use preconfigured jinja2.Environment for autoescape`, whose message states: 'Starlette removed the **env_options passthrough in Jinja2Templates, breaking autoescape=True on newer versions. Pass a preconfigured jinja2.Environment instead, which is the supported API.' Verified against the currently installed Starlette 0.52.1: `Jinja2Templates(directory=..., autoescape=True)` still sets `env.autoescape == True` today (via `env_options.setdefault('autoescape', True)` in `_create_env`), but it now emits `DeprecationWarning: Extra environment options are deprecated. Use a preconfigured jinja2.Environment instead.` None of the project's templates use `| safe`, so autoescape is the sole XSS guard for any user/instance-derived content rendered into these templates. A future Starlette upgrade that finalizes the deprecation would silently stop honoring `autoescape=True` passed this way, re-disabling HTML escaping with no startup error — the same regression class the reverted commit fixed.

findings.title: Template setup now relies on deprecated Starlette env_options
findings.problem: Line 45 passes `autoescape=True` through `Jinja2Templates(..., **env_options)`. In the project environment, Starlette 0.52.1 emits a deprecation warning for extra environment options and says to use a preconfigured `jinja2.Environment` instead. This diff moved away from exactly that supported path. Because `fastapi` is not version-pinned in `pyproject.toml`, a dependency refresh or warnings-as-errors run can turn this import-time template initialization into a failure, blocking every web route that imports this module. Inference: current locked deps still run, but the changed API surface is explicitly marked for removal/deprecation by the framework.
findings.why_it_matters: A dependency refresh or warnings-as-errors run can turn this import-time template initialization into a failure, blocking every web route that imports this module.
findings.fix_hint: Either keep the explicit `jinja2.Environment(loader=jinja2.FileSystemLoader(...), autoescape=True)` and pass it as `env=...`, or drop `autoescape=True` from `Jinja2Templates(directory=...)` since Starlette already defaults autoescape to true.
